title: Java Enum Switch
description: Practice enums and switch expressions with arrow cases.

completionChecks.prompt: What does label hold when priority is Priority.HIGH?
choices: Compilation fails because the switch has no default
completionChecks.explanation: The HIGH constant matches case HIGH, whose arrow value is "now"; a switch that covers every enum constant is exhaustive, so no default is required.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if a new constant URGENT is added to Priority but not to the switch?
choices: Compilation fails because the switch expression is no longer exhaustive
URGENT maps to the first case, later
Nothing changes; switch expressions ignore new constants
completionChecks.explanation: A switch expression must be exhaustive; adding an enum constant that no case handles, with no default, makes it fail to compile until the new constant is covered.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if priority is null when the switch expression executes?
choices: Compilation fails because enum variables cannot be null
completionChecks.explanation: An enum reference can be null, but switching on that null reference throws NullPointerException at runtime.
